results.title: Former Alabama running back makes fifth appearance in ‘NFL Top 100’
results.description: Running back Josh Jacobs has a spot among the “NFL Top 100” for the fifth time in his career. In NFL Films’ 16th annual poll of the league’s players, the Green Bay Packers running back ranked as the 74th-best player...

results.title: NHL Rumors: Ducks Match on Carlsson, Larkin Expands List, Hischier a Hero
results.description: In today’s NHL rumors rundown, the Anaheim Ducks make a franchise-defining decision on Leo Carlsson, Dylan Larkin’s trade situation takes a small but notable turn, and New Jersey Devils captain Nico Hischier is making headlines for something far bigger than hockey.

results.title: Switzerland lineup vs. Argentina: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 quarter-final as Newcastle United target faces race against time
results.description: Sports Mole takes an in-depth look at how Switzerland could line up for Saturday's World Cup 2026 quarter-final against Argentina.

results.title: Albion CSD welcomes new athletic trainer
results.description: Albion CSD is pleased to introduce a new addition to its athletics program. Dylan Jay will assume the role of Athletic Trainer ahead of the fall 2026 season. He was appointed by the Albion Board of Education at the July...

results.title: No way through: Unai Simon has been impossible
results.description: The 29-year-old Spanish goalkeeper hasn’t conceded a goal in his last 609 minutes of action at the FIFA World Cup

results.title: Board expectations and where will Coventry be at Christmas?
results.description: Rob: I definitely think staying in the Premier League is the minimum requirement this season, and in every interview I've seen or heard Doug King do, that's what he's always said. Diane asked: Where do you see us in the Premier League at Christmas? What will shape the Premier League is the sheer volume of European football - nine clubs juggling midweek fixtures, long travel and the reality of three‐game weeks.

results.title: ‘Hopefully we’ll see many more Test matches’ – Harmanpreet Kaur on women’s red-ball future
results.description: ‘Hopefully we’ll see many more Test matches’ – Harmanpreet Kaur on women’s red-ball future (Source: BCCI) India captain Harmanpreet Kaur said players naturally want more opportunities to play Test cricket, but believes the women’s game is moving in the right direction. Since the start of 2021, only four teams, Australia, England, India, and South Africa, [...]
